hypothesis: Incorporating multi-scale SEM imaging data (e.g., combining 500×, 2000×, and 5000× magnification images of the same cathode sample) as multi-resolution input channels within the two-stage transfer learning framework will yield higher classification accuracy and more physically meaningful Grad-CAM attention maps for intermediate aging states (formation and 100-cycle) than single-magnification inputs, because particle-level cracking features visible at low magnification and surface-film microstructural changes induced by electrolyte additives visible only at high magnification provide complementary, non-redundant discriminative signals that a single-scale CNN cannot simultaneously capture.
